title: andropogon scoparius var. genuinus
intro: Andropogon scoparius var. genuinus, also known as bitter broom, is a perennial plant species within the Andropogon scoparius genus, belonging to the grass family Poaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic effects. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, alleviating gastrointestinal issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne due to its antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of bitter broom include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
